items.title: Solo GP Ashley Smith announces second $25M fund to back startups in AI, security and more
items.summary: Vermilion Cliffs Ventures announced Wednesday the close of a $25 million Fund II.

items.title: Apple to produce Made in America wireless chips with Broadcom
items.summary: Apple has signed a $30 billion+ multiyear deal with Broadcom to design and produce more than 15 billion U.S.-made custom wireless connectivity chips for Apple products.

items.title: Venus Aerospace raises $90M Series B to build a new kind of rocket engine
items.summary: Build a new kind of rocket engine and the world will beat a path to your door.

items.title: Blue Origin reportedly raising $10B at $130B valuation
items.summary: Blue Origin is raising $10 billion at a $130 billion pre-money valuation from Coatue Asset Management, Bezos himself, and other big-name investors, The New York Times reported.

items.title: Venus Williams-backed WeWard can now lock your apps until you hit your steps
items.summary: With funding from tennis star and angel investor Venus Williams, the French app WeWard says that it increases walking time by almost 25%.

items.title: Former OpenAI exec Kevin Weil is now on the board of Stoke Space
items.summary: Kevin Weil's new role at Stoke Space suggests reusable rockets are the next hot thing in Silicon Valley.

items.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
items.summary: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

items.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
items.summary: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11 billion valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

items.title: Final extension: Startup Battlefield Australia applications now close July 20
items.summary: If you're building something ambitious, this is a fast track to the people who can move your startup forward.

items.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
items.summary: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

items.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
items.summary: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

items.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
items.summary: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

items.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
items.summary: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

items.title: Google’s Pixel event is set for August 12
items.summary: Google's upcoming event in August will introduce new Pixel devices.

items.title: Figma acquires team behind a vibe-coding app
items.summary: The Y Combinator-backed company started a vibe-coding platform and later built an agent-creation product.

items.title: Netflix dabbles in shorter video content with its new set of publisher deals with Variety, others
items.summary: Netflix is bringing 2- to 20-minute videos to its platform through new partnerships with digital publishers, including Rolling Stone and Variety.

items.title: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: The worst breaches of 2026 so far
items.summary: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.

items.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
items.summary: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

items.title: X adds a video editor to encourage creators to post original content, not stolen reposts
items.summary: X is rolling out a new video editor and recorder for iOS with multilingual captions, green-screen effects, and other editing tools.

items.title: VC firm Chemistry is raising $500M for its second fund
items.summary: Chemistry Ventures, the VC firm launched by Bessemer, Index Ventures, and Andreessen Horowitz alums, is raising $500M for its second fund.
